items.title: Gemini 3 Deep Think: Advancing Science, Research and Engineering — Google Blog
items.author: blog.google
items.snippet: Google DeepMind has been highly active in research publications in early 2026. Key highlights include Gemini Deep Think, a reasoning-enhanced model that is accelerating mathematical and scientific discovery — it has already settled a decade-old conjecture in online submodular optimization and co-authored a paper introducing new families of blow-up solutions to complex fluid dynamics equations, in collaboration with Brown University, NYU, and Stanford. DeepMind also published research on AlphaEvo

items.title: Gemma 4 on Google Developers Blog
items.author: developers.googleblog.com
items.snippet: In April 2026, Google DeepMind has been active with several major announcements. On April 2, Google launched Gemma 4, a family of open models optimized for on-device and edge deployment, accessible via Android's built-in AICore Developer Preview and Google AI Edge. Alongside Gemma 4, Google introduced Agent Skills — one of the first applications to run multi-step autonomous agentic workflows entirely on-device — and Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, an efficiency-focused model delivering 2.5x faster respon

items.title: Tool calling | Genkit - Firebase
items.author: firebase.google.com
items.snippet: Genkit is Google's open-source framework for building AI-powered applications in JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Python. It provides a unified, code-centric approach to building everything from simple text generation to complex multi-step agentic workflows. The framework centers on three core primitives: Flows (typed, observable wrappers around AI logic), Models (a unified API for interacting with LLMs like Gemini, GPT, and Claude), and Tools (functions the LLM can call to interact with external

items.title: From prompt to production: Build full-stack apps faster with Google AI Studio and Firebase
items.author: firebase.blog
items.snippet: Firebase Studio was Google's agentic, cloud-based development environment launched at Cloud Next in April 2025 and sunset on March 19, 2026, when it was replaced by a full-stack vibe coding experience integrated directly into Google AI Studio. During its run, Firebase Studio allowed developers to prototype full-stack apps using natural language prompts via Gemini — including games — without writing code. It supported web, mobile, and backend workloads with one-click Firebase Hosting deployment.

items.title: Releases · google-gemini/deprecated-generative-ai-android
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: The google-gemini GitHub organization remains highly active in early 2026, with the flagship project being gemini-cli — an open-source AI agent that brings Gemini directly into the terminal. The gemini-cli repository publishes nightly releases daily and stable releases weekly (Tuesdays at 20:00 UTC), with the latest nightly tagged at v0.40.0-nightly as of mid-April 2026. The project has also been accepted into Google Summer of Code 2026, signaling continued investment in the open-source ecosyste

items.title: [BUG] v1.20.5 — Conversations Corrupted & Unrecoverable (macOS) - Google AI Developers Forum
items.author: discuss.ai.google.dev
items.snippet: Google Antigravity, Google's new agentic development platform, has been a major focus of AI Studio development in early 2026. Launched in public preview at no cost for individuals, Antigravity combines a traditional AI-powered IDE (Editor View) with an agent-first Manager Surface that lets developers spawn, orchestrate, and observe multiple autonomous agents working across their editor, terminal, and browser simultaneously.
